body.title: [sprint] outcome_check.py reads retired palace DB — migrate to filesystem decision store
body.intention: I intend that outcome_check fires the '/outcome' completion prompt when a decision's spawned_tickets are all closed, by reading the canonical filesystem decision store — verified by a test where an all-closed decision in devlab/runtime/memory/decisions/ triggers the prompt.
body.description:
**Defect (found during the D-agent-capability-mixins-over-domains stream-A sprint, 2026-07-02):** `devlab/claudecode/outcome_check.py` `_decision_palace_node` (around line 44-53) queries the RETIRED Postgres palace (`adc.palace`, `palace.decisions.{decision_id}`) for a decision's spawned_tickets. The canonical store is now the filesystem (`devlab/runtime/memory/decisions/*.json`, D-canonical-memory-consolidation) — so the lookup silently returns None and outcome_check prints nothing even when a decision IS fully shipped. Same class of drift context-load fixed in T-skills-palace-db-to-fs-store. Result: the sprint-ticket Step 11 '/outcome' completion prompt never fires.

**What to change:** replace `_decision_palace_node` (and its psql import) with a filesystem read: grep/scan `devlab/runtime/memory/decisions/*.json` for the record whose `decision_id` (body.decision_id or top-level) matches, prefer the newest non-superseded record by `emitted_at`, and read `spawned_tickets` from its body. Keep `_all_closed` and the print format unchanged. Note the append-only store may hold multiple records for one decision_id (superseded + open) — pick the canonical (open, newest) one, mirroring context-load Step 3's dedupe intent.

**Completion criteria:** given a decision JSON in the filesystem store whose spawned_tickets are ALL closed in the queue, `outcome_check.check(<ticket>)` prints the '🏁 Decision ... is fully shipped — run /outcome ...' line; given one with an open spawned ticket, it prints nothing. No psql / adc.palace reference remains in outcome_check.py.

**Test plan (real red->green):** unit test with a temp/monkeypatched decisions dir containing a decision whose spawned_tickets map to closed queue entries → assert the completion line is printed (capsys); a second case with one open spawned ticket → assert silent. Red against the current palace-DB reader (no DB / no node → silent even when complete), green after the FS migration.

**Validation (runtime):** on the next sprint that closes the LAST ticket of a decision, the sprint-ticket Step 11 surfaces '🏁 Decision D-xxx is fully shipped'. Observable via the printed completion line in the close output.

**Affected files:** `devlab/claudecode/outcome_check.py` (reader swap); NEW `tests/test_outcome_check_fs_store.py`. Reads `devlab/runtime/memory/decisions/`.

**Logging:** none required (read-only CLI helper); the printed completion line IS the observable signal.

**Scope boundary:** migrate the decision-node datasource only; do not change `_all_closed`, the hypothesis extraction, or the print format.
